strategic_profile.executive_summary: Baylor University is a private, R1-classified Christian research university located in Waco, Texas. Blending rigorous academic programs with a deeply rooted Baptist foundation, Baylor is known for its highly structured pre-major tracks in Business, Nursing, and STEM, alongside elite Honors College programs. The university is increasingly selective, focusing heavily on holistic fit, demonstrated faith values, and community contribution.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Baylor's acceptance rate has dropped from nearly 70% pre-pandemic to around 51-52%, reflecting strategic enrollment management and increased applicant volume.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants should no longer treat Baylor as a safe target; strong academic rigor and alignment with the university's Christian mission are essential, especially for impacted pre-majors.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Baylor utilizes a strict 'Pre-Major' system for popular fields like Business, Nursing, and Neuroscience, requiring students to hit high GPA minimums (2.75 - 3.0) in core 'weeder' courses to officially declare.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Admission to the university does not guarantee entry into desired programs. Students must perform exceptionally well in their first 3-4 semesters to avoid being forced into an alternate major.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Despite a high sticker price exceeding $73,000 annually, 91% of undergraduates receive aid, and the 'Baylor Benefit Scholarship' covers full tuition for families earning under $50,000.
analyst_takeaways.implication: The university is aggressive in its discounting to attract high-achieving students, making it financially accessible for both low-income brackets and high-merit candidates.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Baylor integrates Christian commitment directly into its curriculum and student life, including required chapel attendance and religion courses.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students looking for a secular or highly liberal social environment may find the culture stifling, whereas those seeking a supportive, faith-integrated collegiate experience will thrive.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant, traditional campus life heavily influenced by its Baptist affiliation. Features active Greek life, Big 12 athletics (with traditions like the 'Baylor Line'), and a tight-knit community, though some students note a lack of socioeconomic and racial diversity. [cite: 1, 2]
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Situated on a 1,000-acre campus along the Brazos River in Waco, Texas, effectively acting as a mid-point hub between the Dallas-Fort Worth Metroplex and Austin along the I-35 corridor. [cite: 1, 3]
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Classified as an R1 institution ('Very High Research Activity'), providing exceptional undergraduate research opportunities, notably through the highly selective Science Research Fellows program. [cite: 1, 4, 5]

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional policy extended through 2025. Standardized test scores are considered if submitted, but students are reviewed holistically without them. Submission rates remain strong among enrolled students (47% SAT, 22% ACT). [cite: 6, 7]

longitudinal_trends.notes: Data sourced from 2024-2025 Common Data Set and Clastify. Waitlist admit rate calculated based on students who accepted their place on the waitlist. [cite: 8, 9]
Data sourced from 2025-2026 Common Data Set. Waitlist data not publicly disclosed yet for this cycle. [cite: 7]

gpa.notes: Baylor primarily reports unweighted average GPA of admitted students, hovering around 3.74. [cite: 6]

testing.policy_note: Test-optional policy; 47% submitted SAT, 22% submitted ACT for the Fall 2025 entering class. [cite: 6, 7, 10]

colleges.name: Hankamer School of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for ambitious, mathematically competent students interested in high-level consulting, accounting, or professional sales. Must be prepared to maintain strict grade thresholds in 'weeder' foundational courses.
colleges.housing_profile: Often integrated with living-learning centers that emphasize entrepreneurship and professional development.

majors.name: Business (BBA) - All Majors
majors.special_requirements: Students may not earn three or more grades below 'C' in core business courses. Must complete 12 hours in residence before applying to formally enter the Business major. Must successfully transfer by end of third semester. [cite: 11, 12, 13]
majors.notes: Students who fail to meet the 3.0 GPA or fail to pass prerequisites within three semesters are permanently barred from the BBA degree. [cite: 11, 13]

colleges.name: Louise Herrington School of Nursing
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Perfect for resilient students dedicated to healthcare who can handle rigorous science coursework and stringent standardized testing (HESI A2) before their junior year.
colleges.housing_profile: Pre-nursing students live on the Waco campus for their first two years before transitioning to the Dallas campus for clinicals.

majors.name: Nursing
majors.special_requirements: Requires completion of the HESI A2 exam with a score of 80+ in Math and 80+ in English Composite. Must maintain a 2.75 science GPA and 2.75 cumulative GPA. May only repeat one required lower-division science course. [cite: 14, 15, 16]
majors.notes: Students are classified as PNU1 and then PNU2 before formal admission to the nursing school in Dallas. Failing two nursing courses results in dismissal from the major. [cite: 14, 17]

colleges.name: School of Engineering and Computer Science
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Designed for students with a strong foundation in mathematics and logic, looking for direct admission into a highly structured, ABET-accredited curriculum.
colleges.housing_profile: Many ECS students live in the Teal Residential College, an engineering and computer science living-learning community.
colleges.student_archetype: Analytical, technically inclined, and collaborative problem-solvers.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Requires a grade of 'C' or higher in all computer science and mathematics courses used to satisfy the major. [cite: 18, 19]
majors.notes: Direct admit into the major. Students must complete 46 hours of CS courses. Includes robust capstone project and technical writing requirements. [cite: 18, 19]

colleges.name: College of Arts & Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking a broad liberal arts education, or those preparing for medical, dental, or graduate school through rigorous pre-health tracks.
colleges.housing_profile: Science & Health Living-Learning Center is highly popular for pre-health students. [cite: 20]

majors.name: Medical Humanities
majors.special_requirements: Must earn a B or better in MH 1331 and maintain a 3.0 cumulative Baylor GPA. Must qualify before completing 60 hours in residence. [cite: 21]
majors.notes: Unique interdisciplinary program combining science, history, religion, and ethics. Excellent pathway for pre-med and pre-law students. [cite: 20, 21]

majors.name: Psychology
majors.special_requirements: Must complete introductory courses with a C or better and maintain an overall GPA of 2.75 in no fewer than 40 hours attempted at Baylor. Internal transfer requires a 3.30 GPA. [cite: 22, 23]
majors.notes: If requirements are not met, students are forced to change to 'Undeclared'. Note that internal transfers into Pre-PSY BS face a steep 3.3 GPA requirement compared to standard pre-majors. [cite: 22]

supplemental_requirements.details: One 450-word essay. Prompt: 'What are you looking for in a university, why do you want to attend Baylor, and how do you see yourself contributing to the Baylor community?' Optional for EA/RD, but REQUIRED for Early Decision applicants. Effectively acts as a 'Why Us' and 'Community' dual essay. [cite: 26, 27]

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Baylor strongly values legacy connections (31% of the freshman class are legacies) and distinct alignment with Christian values, heavily emphasizing fit for their tight-knit campus. [cite: 2, 28]

major_selection_tactics: Apply to non-impacted majors in the College of Arts & Sciences if STEM or Business metrics are weak, then attempt an internal transfer.

Utilize the alternate major option on the application; Baylor explicitly asks for alternate majors to offer admission to high-quality candidates who don't meet the strict cutoffs for impacted programs like Nursing or CS. [cite: 29]
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Crucial. Impacted majors like Pre-Nursing, Pre-Business, and Computer Science have stringent admission criteria. Listing a less impacted secondary major (e.g., Medical Humanities instead of Biology/Nursing) ensures you aren't completely rejected if you fall just short of the primary major's threshold. [cite: 29, 30]

financials.tuition_model: Flat-rate tuition model for 12-18 credit hours per semester.

scholarships.name: Regents' Gold Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers full tuition and an additional stipend depending on SAT/ACT scores. Requires maintaining a 3.5 Baylor GPA.

scholarships.name: President's / Provost's / Deans' Gold Scholarships
scholarships.benefits: Merit-based scholarships divided evenly over four years. Requires maintaining a 3.0 cumulative GPA to renew after freshman year.

scholarships.name: Baylor Benefit Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers tuition and fees for selected incoming students with demonstrated financial need and an adjusted household income of $50,000 or less.

scholarships.name: Faculty Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers full tuition, room, and board. Highly competitive.

scholarships.name: Carr P. Collins Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Awarded to top Texas residents with strong SAT/ACT scores and class rank. Must meet specific preparatory curriculum requirements.

ap_policy.general_rule: Baylor accepts AP credits, but generally limits credits applied toward degree progression or uses them as generic electives rather than major-specific fulfillment for competitive Pre-Health tracks. [cite: 31]
ap_policy.usage: Credit is awarded but does not apply to the Baylor GPA. Dual credit/transfer credit must be a 'C' or higher. [cite: 7]

ib_policy.general_rule: IB policies are strict and mirror AP policies regarding departmental review and GPA calculation.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Students must internally request official evaluations. Baylor does not count transfer credits toward the Baylor institutional GPA, which makes it challenging for internal transfer pathways that require a high Baylor GPA (like Pre-Business or Pre-Nursing). [cite: 7, 32]

what_it_takes: Unweighted GPA in the top 25% of the graduating class.
Strong alignment with Baylor's Christian mission and values.
Demonstrated interest (via submitting the optional essay).
common_activities: Running the Baylor Line (Freshman football tradition)
essay_tips: Break the 450-word prompt into 'What I want', 'Why Baylor', and 'How I will contribute'.
Treat the essay as a combined 'Why Us' and 'Community' essay to maximize the limited word count.
Be specific about unique programs like the Honors College, BIC, or specific Living-Learning Centers. [cite: 26, 27, 33]
red_flags: Applying Early Decision without submitting the mandatory supplemental essay.
Applying to Pre-Nursing or Pre-Business with low high school grades in foundational math or science courses. [cite: 26]
insights: The 'Pre-Major' system is effectively a weed-out process; students who fail to maintain the 2.75-3.0 threshold in their first three semesters are dropped to 'Undeclared' and forced to change fields. [cite: 11, 21, 22]
Baylor accepts an enormous waitlist class but admits very few from it. In 2024, they admitted only 7.1% of those offered a spot on the waitlist. [cite: 8, 34]
